message:
chore(beads): close 4 audit beads resolved by this REVIEW sweep (FROST deserialize, X-Principal threading, gossip cap, terraform handshake)

Closes the four bead records resolved by the preceding four
commits in this sweep. Each bead's status line flips from `"open"`
to `"closed"` and its close_reason points at the resolving
commit. Line count unchanged (7 lines modified, 0 insertions, 0
deletions) — existing issue bodies are preserved verbatim, only
the status/close_reason/closed_at metadata fields update.

Resolved beads and their landing commits:

- `flywheel_connectors-5mhhv` / `flywheel_connectors-5zmkr`
  [REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-crypto: FrostSigningNonces still duplicate
  via serde round-trip → closed by `harden(fcp-crypto): block
  FrostSigningNonces deserialize to close serde nonce-duplication
  hole` (commit 9e0fe660).
- `flywheel_connectors-9ftlo` [REVIEW][MED] fcp-host: preflight
  and simulate ignore X-Principal binding enforced by invoke →
  closed by `fix(fcp-host): thread X-Principal header through
  preflight and simulate handlers` (commit 746a1e38).
- `flywheel_connectors-qc3d7` [REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-mesh:
  dispatch_gossip_payload parses unbounded JSON before gossip
  caps → closed by `fix(fcp-mesh): reject oversized gossip
  payloads before serde_json::from_slice` (commit 0f531341).
- `flywheel_connectors-619oy` [REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-e2e/terraform:
  adapter fabricates handshake response instead of verifying
  connector contract → closed by `fix(fcp-e2e/terraform): drive
  real HandshakeRequest through connector + assert response
  contract` (commit d18041eb).

One additional bead's status line is also re-synced in this
commit via the same `br sync` operation (net zero content
change); this is routine metadata reconciliation that landed in
the same file-level save.

The remaining open REVIEW beads from this sweep (br-2h9au / br-
w1098 fcp-cbor canonicalize cap, br-9sztj / br-a6ouw fcp-e2e/
spotify handshake, br-tlspb fcp-store coverage locks, br-u2g49 /
br-wtp2t fcp-e2e/webhook_receiver listing coverage) are
deliberately left open — each needs its own fix commit, and
bundling them with this close would conflate "verified
resolution" with "tracked follow-up".
